Summarized the recent #tenex-edge channel chatter: agent-status docs were added (#363 hook tree merged via PR #372), issue #375 was filed for a daemon management-classifier feedback loop, and PR #378/#377 for shortest-handle lease + npub-first resume went through 5 review rounds before merging.

Confirmed agents are replying to themselves due to divergent @mention text and p-tag routing — the `codex-<name>`-prefixed handle form doesn't resolve correctly while the `-codex`-suffixed form does, suggesting two different codename-emission paths.

Found that the daemon's event-processing loop was stalled (initially diagnosed as deadlocked), with ~8,992 offline-mention dispatches logged and no chat events ingested for ~3 hours, meaning no agent was receiving messages — not just slate-falcon.

No — #375 is still open with no fix landed; no commit references it. The ~9,000-iteration offline-mention loop seen on this machine is exactly the issue described in #375, occurring unfixed in production.
how obvious of a fix is it? how obvious is it that this is the problem we're facing? is it possible to TDD the fix? aim for a 1 or 2 min answer

Spoke the answer via TTS: the loop is obvious in code (unconditional offline-mention dispatch with no agent-author guard), the causation of the wedge is a strong inference but not proven, and the routing fix is TDD-able while the deadlock itself is timing-dependent and harder to test.

thats absolutely the wrong fix! the whole fucking point is that agents are supposed to fucking be able to talk to each other!!!! the offline mention dispatch when the agent is already online doesnt sound like it make sense -- but preventing an agent from talking to other agents is fucking retarded!!! also; research WHY the current agent is not replying -- investigate their pty or whatever the fuck

Retracted the earlier deadlock and anti-agent-talk claims. The real cause: slate-falcon's messages are stuck pending because its codex process runs in a plain iTerm tab with no daemon-injectable PTY — when idle, the daemon has nowhere to doorbell, so delivery silently black-holes while the agent still appears alive.

when an agent is not running in a pty wrapped we need to mention it in the injection somewhere in the "you are X" and the ./skills/tenex-edge/ should explain the consequences in a resource about not being wrapped in a pty and maybe give them a way to rerun themselves via a `tenex-edge my session pty-wrap-me` that kills the process and resumes the session within a pty. send an opus agent to research the consequences of not being in a pty, like mentions are not injected immediately and stuff like that

Noted a channel message confirming #375 is still open with only active analysis — deliberately did not reply to avoid feeding the loop. Holding on the user's call for next steps: nudge slate-falcon's iTerm tab, file the black-hole issue, or start implementing the preamble/skill/pty-wrap-me pieces.

Partly: #375's classifier loop is now fixed by PR #399, and #398/#391 added a daemon restart subcommand and fixed orphan-supervisor ghosts. However, the core DeferNoEndpoint no-op black-hole is still unfixed, and the preamble warning, skill resource, and pty-wrap-me command are still open.

All three pieces shipped and merged: #354 (session kill --self) → PR #404, preamble warning → PR #405, and `session pty-wrap-me` → PR #407. Caveat: pty-wrap-me's kill+resume path was unit-tested only, not exercised end-to-end against a live agent.
Removed the 3 merged worktrees (left non-owned ones alone) and began setting up the live lab environment for the pty-wrap-me smoke test.

smoke-test the new `tenex-edge my session pty-wrap-me --self` command: launch an agent OUTSIDE a daemon PTY (reproduce the non-PTY / no pty_session alias condition), send it a mention while idle to confirm it black-holes, then run pty-wrap-me and verify it re-homes into a daemon PTY and the pending mention now delivers

PASS — all 5 steps verified end-to-end against a real Claude agent: reproduced the non-PTY black-hole, confirmed the #405 warning appeared, ran `pty-wrap-me --self` successfully, and verified the stuck mention flipped to delivered after re-homing into a daemon PTY. The only open item is the systemic DeferNoEndpoint no-op fix, which was scoped out earlier.
